q: As I listened to music, I studied English.
o: 1. Listening to music, I studied English.
2. Listened to music, I studied English.

q: As I listened to music, I studied English.
o: 1. Listening to music, I studied English.
2. Having listened to music, I studied English.

q: While you surf the Internet, you can take a trip around the world.
o: 1. Surfing the Internet, you can take a trip around the world.
2. Playing the Internet, you can take a trip around the world.

q: I like to study as I listen to pop music.
o: 1. I like to study, listening to pop music.
2. We like to study, listening to pop music.

q: If you drink lots of water, you will get health.
o: 1. Drinking lots of water, you will get health.
2. Drunking lots of water, you will get health.

q: Though I was sick, I had to take the test.
o: 1. Being sick, I had to take the test.
2. Wasing sick, I had to take the test.

q: Because I had no money, I had to go home on foot.
o: 1. Having no money, I had to go home on foot.
2. Having had no money, I had to go home on foot.

q: As Rick didn't feel hungry, Rick didn't have lunch.
o: 1. Feeling hungry, Rick didn't have lunch.
2. Not feeling hungry, Rick didn't have lunch.

q: When you don't play computer games, you can study English.
o: 1. Playing computer games, you can study English.
2. Not playing computer games, you can study English.

q: When I walked down the street, I happened to  meet Tony.
o: 1. Walking down the street, I happened to  meet Tony.
2. Walked down the street, I happened to  meet Tony.

q: Because I didn't have any money, I had to go home on foot.
o: 1. Having any money, I had to go home on foot.
2. Not having any money, I had to go home on foot.

q: Though he is very rich, he never helps the poor.
o: 1. Being very rich, he never helps the poor.
2. Not being very rich, he never helps the poor.

q: Though he isn't very rich, he always helps the poor.
o: 1. Being very rich, he never helps the poor.
2. Not being very rich, he always helps the poor.

q: When he saw a policeman, he ran away.
o: 1. Seeing a policeman, he ran away.
2. Sawing a policeman, he ran away.

q: As he felt hungry, he ate apples.
o: 1. Feeling hungry, he ate apples.
2. Felting hungry, he ate apples.

q: Though he didn't feel hungry, he ate apples.
o: 1. Feeling hungry, he ate apples.
2. Not feeling hungry, he ate apples.

q: As I was ill, I could not go out.
o: 1. Being ill, I could not go out.
2. Wasing ill, I could not go out.

q: If you take this bus, you'll get there on time.
o: 1. Taking this bus, you'll get there on time.
2. Being this bus, you'll get there on time.

q: Although I live near the sea, I cannot swim.
o: 1. Living near the sea, I cannot swim.
2. Not living near the sea, I cannot swim.

q: Although I don't live near the sea, I can swim.
o: 1. Living near the sea, I cannot swim.
2. Not living near the sea, I can swim.
